steps.title: Capture the full argument
steps.content: The passage contrasts an earlier homology hypothesis with Aureliano et al.’s findings. The team examined fossils of common ancestors, found openings whose sizes did not support PSP, and reported other findings against homology. Their evidence contradicted the idea that PSP came from a shared ancestor and led them to conclude that PSP likely evolved independently in each clade.

steps.title: Avoid narrow or unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A focuses on one detail rather than the whole conclusion. Choice B invents a technology limitation for earlier researchers. Choice C says PSP evolved independently in pterosaurs and was then inherited by other clades, which contradicts “independently in each clade.” The remaining choice captures the rejected common-ancestor idea and the different evolutionary origin. The answer is D.
